question: [File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit a race condition in AccountBalancesDownloader.shouldDownload() where enabled=false causes the method to check accountBalanceFileExists.get() and then accountBalanceFileRepository.findLatest(), and by flooding the database with fake account_balance_file records (if write access to the DB is obtained via SQL injection elsewhere), cause findLatest() to return stale or incorrect data, causing shouldDownload() to return false and halt balance downloads on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getStreamType()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that getStreamType() always returns StreamType.BALANCE (hardcoded) to craft a scenario where the balance stream type is confused with another stream type in a multi-tenant importer deployment, causing the DateRangeFilter in AccountBalanceFileParser.doParse() to reject all balance files as out-of-range, halting balance ingestion on >= 30% of nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_CONSENSUS_RATIO=1 via environment variable, causing CommonDownloaderProperties.consensusRatio to require 100% of all nodes to agree on a balance file signature, and since any single node being offline prevents consensus, effectively halting balance file processing on all importer nodes whenever any network node is unavailable?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_SOURCES to a list of attacker-controlled S3-compatible endpoints via environment variable, causing CommonDownloaderProperties.sources to be populated with malicious sources that return crafted responses, causing the Flux-based parallel download in Downloader.downloadAndParseSigFiles() to block indefinitely on attacker-controlled slow responses, exhausting the parallel scheduler thread pool on >= 30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getFrequency()] Can an unprivileged external user exploit the fact that BalanceDownloaderProperties.frequency has @NotNull but no @DurationMin constraint (unlike CommonDownloaderProperties.pathRefreshInterval and timeout which both have @DurationMin(seconds=1)), to set frequency=PT0.000000001S (1 nanosecond) which rounds to 0ms, causing the Spring scheduler to schedule download() with a 0ms fixed delay and create a CPU-saturating tight loop on >= 30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: isEnabled()] Can an unprivileged external user who can modify a shared Helm chart values.yaml (e.g., via a compromised CI/CD pipeline) set enabled=true for balance downloader while the historicalBalance parser is also enabled, causing HistoricalBalanceProperties.init() to throw IllegalArgumentException('The two configuration properties can't be both true') during Spring context initialization, preventing >= 30% of importer nodes from starting up?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_SOURCES_MAX_CONCURRENCY=1 via environment variable, reducing the maximum number of allowed open HTTP connections to 1, causing the parallel Flux-based signature file download in Downloader.downloadAndParseSigFiles() to serialize all node downloads, creating a bottleneck that causes the download timeout to be exceeded and halting balance processing on >= 30% of importer nodes?

[File: 'File Name: importer/src/main/java/org/hiero/mirror/importer/downloader/balance/BalanceDownloaderProperties.java -> Scope: Medium: Shutdown of greater than or equal to 30% of network processing nodes without brute force actions, but does not shut down the network'] [Function: getCommon()] Can an unprivileged external user set HIERO_MIRROR_IMPORTER_DOWNLOADER_SOURCES_BACKOFF=0s via environment variable (bypassing any minimum constraint), causing the source backoff after an exception to be 0 seconds, resulting in a tight retry loop that floods the cloud storage endpoint with requests and triggers rate limiting (HTTP 429), which then causes all balance file downloads to fail on >= 30% of importer nodes?
